rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA VAN HOY et al VS. ALFA LAVAL INC.(SUED INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 28, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Crane Co.'s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution pursuant to C.C.P. 583.410 and 583.420 is denied without prejudice as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice as required by CRC 3.1342 and C.C.P. 1010.6. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD L. BASS et al VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 28, 2014 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Puget Sound Commerce Center, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Donald Bass was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Decedent's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Decedent's deposition testimony and Dr. Shonfeld's declaration create a triable issue whether exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to decedent's asbestos-related illnesses. Assuming defendant successfully demonstrated that it cannot be liable under theories of retained control or concealed dangerous conditions, defendant did not demonstrate that such conclusions bar plaintiffs' claim for premises owner/contractor liability. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD KOEPKE et al VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 28, 2014 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Kelsey-Hayes Company's motion for summary judgment or, alternatively, summary adjudication: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)
